summary: This cluster investigates the ecological roles of dinoflagellate communities in hypoxic marine environments, specifically within the bottom waters of Jinhae Bay and Masan Bay, Korea. Through metabarcoding analyses, researchers documented a significant increase in the abundance of heterotrophic dinoflagellate species under low-oxygen conditions, identifying 36 species spanning autotrophic, mixotrophic, and heterotrophic trophic modes. The findings establish a foundational understanding of how these protists contribute to nutrient cycling and trophic dynamics in oxygen-depleted zones, emphasizing the utility of metabarcoding for characterizing microbial eukaryotic diversity in such stressed ecosystems.
